cli

Class ShortFlagHandler

  • java.lang.Object
    • cli.ShortFlagHandler
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    FlagHandler

    public class ShortFlagHandler
extends java.lang.Object
implements FlagHandler
    Provides a concrete strategy for processing short-form flags based on POSIX conventions.

    This handler acts as a lexical processor designed to identify short flags, prefixed by a single dash (-), and extracts their associated values from the token stream. It supports multiple standard formats:

    • Clustered: -vh (Equivalent to -v -h)
    • Attached/Glued: -p8080 (Where 8080 is the argument for p)
    • Explicit Separator: -o=file.txt

    Upon identifying a flag, the handler notifies the FlagListener using the Observer design pattern. By passing the flag's name and its associated value (if any), the handler effectively decouples lexical extraction from state management and validation logic.

    Since:
    2 March 2026
    Version:
    1.1
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

      void handle(common.PeekingIterator<java.lang.String> tokens, FlagRegistry registry, FlagListener observer)
      Processes a short-form flag and its potential arguments by consuming tokens from the iterator provided.

    • Method Detail

      • handle

        public void handle(common.PeekingIterator<java.lang.String> tokens,
                   FlagRegistry registry,
                   FlagListener observer)
        Processes a short-form flag and its potential arguments by consuming tokens from the iterator provided.
        Specified by:
        handle in interface FlagHandler
        Parameters:
        tokens - the iterator over normalised tokens, supporting look-ahead operations
        registry - the registry containing the valid FlagRule definitions
        observer - the event-driven listener notified when a flag or unrecognised token is identified
